title: Chapter 1: Greetings & The Verb 'Sein'
description: Learn to say hello and introduce yourself using the verb 'sein' (to be). High repetition for internalization.

sentences.english: Have a nice day! (Beautiful day still!)

title: Chapter 2: Essential Verbs (Kommen, Wohnen, Heißen)
description: Learn to talk about where you are from and where you live. High repetition for internalization.

sentences.english: What is your name? (How are you called?)
No, my name is not Lukas.

sentences.german: Nein, ich komme nicht aus Berlin.
sentences.english: No, I don't come from Berlin.

sentences.german: Nein, ich wohne nicht in München.
sentences.english: No, I don't live in Munich.

sentences.german: Ich bin Student und wohne in Berlin.
sentences.english: I am a student and live in Berlin.

sentences.german: Er heißt Thomas und kommt aus Spanien.
sentences.english: His name is Thomas and he comes from Spain.

sentences.german: Sie heißt Maria und wohnt in Rom.
sentences.english: Her name is Maria and she lives in Rome.
Where do your friends come from?
Do your friends live in Berlin?
Today I am staying in a hotel.

sentences.german: Ich heiße Anna und komme aus der Schweiz.
sentences.english: My name is Anna and I come from Switzerland.

title: Chapter 3: Nouns & Genders (Nominative)
description: Learn German genders (der, die, das) and simple adjectives in the nominative case. High repetition for internalization.

sentences.german: Der Apfel ist auf dem Tisch.
sentences.english: The apple is on the table.

title: Chapter 4: Possession (Haben) & The Accusative Case
description: Learn how to talk about things you have. Introducing the accusative case (den, einen). High repetition.

sentences.english: Do you have a mobile phone?

title: Chapter 5: Present Tense Verbs (Regular)
description: Learn common regular verbs like machen, sagen, fragen, and hören. High repetition.

sentences.english: He is listening to the radio.
I am looking for the key.
Are you looking for your mobile phone?

title: Chapter 6: Negation (nicht vs. kein)
description: Master the difference between negating with 'nicht' and 'kein'. High repetition.

sentences.english: Do you have no mobile phone?
He is not a good friend.
That is not a good idea.
He is not telling the truth.
She is not buying a car.

title: Chapter 7: Locations & Prepositions (Dative intro)
description: Learn to describe where you are and where you are going. High repetition of 'in', 'auf', 'zu', 'nach'.

sentences.german: Die Bank ist in der Nähe.

sentences.english: We are going to the cinema.
Are you going to the restaurant?

sentences.german: Das Buch ist auf dem Tisch.
sentences.english: The book is on the table.

sentences.german: Das Handy ist in der Tasche.
sentences.english: The mobile phone is in the pocket.
I am at my friend's house.
I am waiting at the stop.
The train is at the station.
Do you live in the city?

sentences.german: Nein, ich wohne auf dem Land.
sentences.english: No, I live in the country.
The dog is running in the garden.
The children are playing in the park.
We are almost at the destination.

title: Chapter 8: Plurals & Numbers
description: Learn plural forms and how to count. Note that the article for all plurals is 'die'. High repetition.

sentences.english: How many children do you have?

sentences.german: Drei Kinder sind in der Schule.
Die Eier sind in der Küche.
sentences.english: The eggs are in the kitchen.
Do you also have friends here?

sentences.german: Wie viele Zimmer hat das Haus?
sentences.english: How many rooms does the house have?

title: Chapter 9: The Dative Case (Indirect Objects)
description: Learn how the dative case works for indirect objects and personal pronouns (mir, dir, etc.). High repetition.

sentences.english: How are you? (How goes it to you?)
I am fine. (To me goes it well.)
Do you give me the book?
I am writing you a message.

sentences.german: Er schreibt seiner Mutter einen Brief.
sentences.english: He is writing his mother a letter.
Will you bring me the water?
Will you buy me an ice cream?
I am giving you a book (as a gift).
I am cold. (To me is cold.)
We are bored. (To us is boring.)
I am sorry. (That does to me sorrow.)
I like the taste of that very much.

title: Chapter 10: Separable Verbs
description: German verbs with prefixes that move to the end (e.g., aufstehen, anrufen). High repetition.

sentences.german: Er steht um sieben Uhr auf.
sentences.english: He gets up at seven o'clock.
He is coming along to the cinema.

sentences.german: Bitte machen Sie die Tür zu.

sentences.english: We watch TV in the evening.
Are you getting off at the station?
I am taking off my jacket.
I am taking out the trash.
I am cleaning/tidying up my room.

sentences.german: Ich höre mit dem Rauchen auf.
Ich sehe mir das Foto an.
sentences.english: I am looking at the photo.
I am taking the book with me.
Are you taking me with you?
I learn something new every day.

title: Chapter 11: The Perfect Tense (Speaking about the Past)
description: Learn how to use 'haben' or 'sein' with the past participle to talk about the past. High repetition.

sentences.english: I ate an apple. (I have eaten an apple.)
I drove to Berlin. (I am driven...)
I love you / I'm fond of you.
I made it! / I did it!

title: Chapter 12: Question Words & Comprehensive Review
description: Master all question words (W-Fragen) and review all concepts. High repetition.

sentences.english: How many children do you have?

sentences.german: Kommst du aus Berlin oder München?
sentences.english: Do you come from Berlin or Munich?
I am waiting for the bus.
I have the day off today.
Do you have time on the weekend? (formal)
I am going for a walk in the park.
Do you like going for walks?
I'm glad to hear that. (That pleases me.)

sentences.german: Ich wünsche dir einen schönen Tag.
sentences.english: I wish you a nice day.
